title: Pokedex Screenplay
description: Schema for exploratory test screenplay YAML files. Screenplays define a sequence of CLI commands and assertions to replay as regression tests.

needs_seed.description: Whether this screenplay requires a seeded database

mutates_collection.description: Whether any step modifies collection data (add/update/remove)

name.description: Human-readable description of what this step tests

command.description: The CLI command to execute. Use $variable for captured values from previous steps. No shell quoting — arguments are split on whitespace.

capture.description: Extract values from the JSON response for use in subsequent steps via $variable substitution

exit_code.description: Expected process exit code. 0 for success, 1 for errors.

has_fields.description: List of dot-paths that must exist and be non-null in the JSON response

items.description: Dot-path (e.g., 'data.types', 'meta.command', 'actions'). Array indices use numeric segments (e.g., 'data.0.name').

equals.description: Map of dot-path to expected value. Value can be string, number, boolean, null, or array.

contains.description: Map of dot-path to substring. Checks that the value at the path contains the substring. For non-string values, checks the JSON serialization.

array_len.description: Map of dot-path to bounds. Checks that the array at the path has length within bounds.

type_of.description: Map of dot-path to expected JSON type.
